question: [File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: liquidate() + ERC777 collateralToken + tokensReceived hook reenters liquidate()] Can an unprivileged liquidator under a market whose collateralToken is ERC777 trigger a tokensReceived hook during safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, seizedAssets) (line 696) that reenters liquidate() for the same borrower, so that the second liquidation executes against the already-updated state (collateral and debt already reduced at lines 670-676) and may realize bad debt or seize remaining collateral a second time, violating the invariant that each liquidation must operate on consistent pre-call state, causing scoped impact: double-liquidation of the same position leading to incorrect bad debt socialization or over-seizure? Proof idea: deploy ERC777 collateralToken; receiver's tokensReceived calls liquidate() again; assert borrower.debt and borrower.collateral are consistent after both calls.

[File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: repay() + ERC777 loanToken + tokensToSend hook reenters repay()] Can an unprivileged borrower under a market whose loanToken is ERC777 trigger a tokensToSend hook during safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), units) (line 520) that reenters repay() for the same onBehalf and units, so that position[id][onBehalf].debt is decremented twice (lines 508 in both calls) and marketState[id].withdrawable is incremented twice (line 509 in both calls) while only one actual token transfer completes (the outer one), violating the invariant that withdrawable <= loanToken.balanceOf(midnight), causing scoped impact: withdrawable is inflated by units without a corresponding token deposit, creating insolvency? Proof idea: deploy ERC777 loanToken; hook calls repay() again during tokensToSend; assert withdrawable > loanToken.balanceOf(midnight) after both calls.

[File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: take() + IBuyCallback + fee-on-transfer loanToken + payer is callback] Can an unprivileged maker under a buy offer with a buyerCallback set so that payer = buyerCallback (line 422) call take() with a fee-on-transfer loanToken so that safeTransferFrom(loanToken, buyerCallback, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) (line 455) delivers less than buyerAssets - sellerAssets to the protocol while claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] is incremented by the full buyerAssets - sellerAssets (line 418), violating the invariant that claimableSettlementFee[token] <= actual surplus balance, causing scoped impact: settlement fee accounting is overcounted when the payer is a callback contract with a fee-on-transfer token? Proof idea: deploy fee-on-transfer loanToken and IBuyCallback as payer; execute take(); assert claimableSettlementFee > actual balance surplus.

[File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: multicall() + supplyCollateral() + fee-on-transfer collateralToken] Can an unprivileged borrower under a fee-on-transfer collateralToken market use multicall([supplyCollateral, take_sell_offer, withdrawCollateral]) in a single transaction so that supplyCollateral() records assets in position.collateral[i] (line 533) but only receives assets*(1-fee_rate), then take() passes isHealthy() using the inflated collateral value, and withdrawCollateral() releases the full recorded assets, violating the invariant that collateral withdrawn <= collateral actually deposited, causing scoped impact: borrower extracts more collateral than deposited via multicall atomicity with fee-on-transfer token? Proof idea: deploy fee-on-transfer collateralToken; multicall([supplyCollateral(1e18), take(debt), withdrawCollateral(1e18)]); assert protocol collateral balance goes negative.

[File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: take() + ISellCallback + fee-on-transfer loanToken + receiver gets less] Can an unprivileged taker under a sell offer with a fee-on-transfer loanToken call take() so that safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, receiver, sellerAssets) (line 456) delivers only sellerAssets*(1-fee_rate) to receiver while the seller's position.credit is reduced by sellerCreditDecrease units at face value (line 413), violating the invariant that the seller receives assets equal to sellerCreditDecrease units * sellerPrice / WAD, causing scoped impact: maker/seller loses fee_rate percent of their asset proceeds with no protocol compensation or recourse? Proof idea: fuzz sellerAssets; assert receiver.balanceOf after take < sellerAssets; assert seller credit correctly reduced.

[File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: take() + fee-on-transfer loanToken + settlement fee breakpoint boundary] Can an unprivileged taker under a fee-on-transfer loanToken market execute take() at a settlement fee breakpoint (timeToMaturity in {0, 1 day, 7 days, 30 days, 90 days, 180 days, 360 days}) where buyerAssets - sellerAssets is maximized, so that the fee-on-transfer loss on the settlement fee portion is maximized, violating the invariant that claimableSettlementFee[token] <= actual balance surplus, causing scoped impact: insolvency is worst at maximum settlement fee breakpoints? Proof idea: fuzz timeToMaturity across all 7 breakpoints; deploy fee-on-transfer token; assert insolvency gap is proportional to settlement fee * fee_rate.

[File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: repay() + fee-on-transfer loanToken + bad debt socialization] Can an unprivileged liquidator under a fee-on-transfer loanToken market first inflate marketState[id].withdrawable via repay() (fee-on-transfer underpays), then trigger bad debt socialization via liquidate() (line 626-641) which computes lossFactor using the inflated totalUnits, so that the lossFactor is computed on a totalUnits value that exceeds actual loanToken balance, violating the invariant that lossFactor correctly reflects the ratio of bad debt to real recoverable assets, causing scoped impact: bad debt socialization is miscalibrated, lenders lose more or less credit than the actual bad debt warrants? Proof idea: repay with fee-on-transfer token N times; create bad debt; liquidate; assert lossFactor * totalUnits / WAD != actual bad debt.

[File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: take() + ERC20NoReturn collateralToken in supplyCollateral()] Can an unprivileged borrower under a market whose collateralToken omits the return value on transferFrom (ERC20NoReturn pattern) call supplyCollateral() so that SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom checks returndata.length == 0 (line 33 of SafeTransferLib) and accepts the transfer as successful even if the token's internal logic silently fails without reverting, violating the invariant that position.collateral[i] reflects actual tokens received, causing scoped impact: if the token silently no-ops the transfer, the borrower gets phantom collateral credit? Proof idea: deploy ERC20NoReturn that no-ops transferFrom but returns no data; call supplyCollateral(); assert position.collateral[i] > 0 but collateralToken.balanceOf(midnight) == 0.

[File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: take() + ERC20NoRevert loanToken] Can an unprivileged taker under a market whose loanToken silently no-ops transferFrom without reverting (ERC20NoRevert pattern) call take() so that SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) (line 455) succeeds (no revert, empty returndata accepted) while the protocol receives 0 tokens, violating the invariant that loanToken.balanceOf(midnight) increases by buyerAssets - sellerAssets after take(), causing scoped impact: settlement fee is credited to claimableSettlementFee without any actual token transfer, creating insolvency? [File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: repay() + ERC20NoRevert loanToken] Can an unprivileged borrower under a market whose loanToken silently no-ops transferFrom without reverting call repay(market, units, onBehalf, address(0), '') so that SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), units) (line 520) succeeds while the protocol receives 0 tokens, violating the invariant that withdrawable <= loanToken.balanceOf(midnight), causing scoped impact: borrower repays debt and frees withdrawable without transferring any tokens, allowing lenders to drain the protocol's existing balance? [File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: supplyCollateral() + ERC20NoRevert collateralToken] Can an unprivileged borrower under a market whose collateralToken silently no-ops transferFrom without reverting call supplyCollateral(market, collateralIndex, assets, onBehalf) so that SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom(collateralToken, msg.sender, address(this), assets) (line 545) succeeds while the protocol receives 0 collateral tokens, violating the invariant that position.collateral[i] <= collateralToken.balanceOf(midnight), causing scoped impact: borrower obtains phantom collateral credit, passes isHealthy() checks, and borrows loanToken against non-existent collateral? [File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: liquidate() + ERC20NoRevert loanToken] Can an unprivileged liquidator under a market whose loanToken silently no-ops transferFrom without reverting call liquidate() so that SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), repaidUnits) (line 717) succeeds while the protocol receives 0 tokens, violating the invariant that withdrawable <= loanToken.balanceOf(midnight), causing scoped impact: liquidator seizes collateral and reduces borrower debt without paying any loanToken, draining collateral for free and inflating withdrawable? [File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: take() + ISellCallback.onSell() returns wrong bytes32] Can an unprivileged taker under a sell offer with a sellerCallback that returns a non-CALLBACK_SUCCESS bytes32 cause take() to revert with WrongSellCallbackReturnValue (lines 460-473) after both safeTransferFrom calls (lines 455-456) have already executed, but since the whole tx reverts the transfers are rolled back, violating the liveness invariant that a valid sell offer must be takeable, causing scoped impact: any sell offer whose callback returns an incorrect magic value permanently blocks all takes against that offer? [File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: take() + fee-on-transfer loanToken + offer.buy=false + receiver is offer.receiverIfMakerIsSeller] Can an unprivileged taker under a sell offer (offer.buy=false) with a fee-on-transfer loanToken call take() so that safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, offer.receiverIfMakerIsSeller, sellerAssets) (line 456) delivers only sellerAssets*(1-fee_rate) to the maker's designated receiver while the maker's credit is reduced by sellerCreditDecrease units at face value, violating the invariant that the maker receives the full sellerAssets they are owed, causing scoped impact: maker systematically receives less than the agreed price for their sell offer with no protocol-level protection? [File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: take() + fee-on-transfer loanToken + continuousFee accrual] Can an unprivileged taker under a fee-on-transfer loanToken market execute take() that increases buyerCreditIncrease, so that buyerPendingFeeIncrease is computed as buyerCreditIncrease.mulDivDown(continuousFee * timeToMaturity, WAD) (line 386) based on the full credit increase while the actual loanToken received by the protocol is reduced by fee_rate, violating the invariant that pendingFee obligations are backed by actual token balances, causing scoped impact: pendingFee accrual is overcounted relative to actual protocol balance, compounding insolvency with settlement fee overcounting? [File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: flashLoan() + false-return token] Can an unprivileged flash loan caller under a market whose token returns false on transfer call flashLoan([token], [assets], callback, data) so that SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(token, callback, assets) (line 743) reverts with TransferReturnedFalse, violating the liveness invariant that flashLoan must always be callable for tokens held by the protocol, causing scoped impact: permanent flash loan freeze if any token in the tokens array returns false on transfer? [File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: take() + IBuyCallback.onBuy() + fee-on-transfer loanToken + callback sources funds] Can an unprivileged maker under a buy offer with a buyerCallback that sources loanToken during onBuy() call take() with a fee-on-transfer loanToken so that the callback receives buyerAssets from the payer (via the callback's own transfer logic) but the subsequent safeTransferFrom(loanToken, buyerCallback, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) (line 455) delivers only (buyerAssets - sellerAssets)*(1-fee_rate) to the protocol, violating the invariant that the protocol receives the full settlement fee, causing scoped impact: settlement fee is systematically underpaid when the callback is the payer and the loanToken is fee-on-transfer? Proof idea: deploy fee-on-transfer loanToken and IBuyCallback that sources funds; assert claimableSettlementFee > actual balance surplus after take.

[File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: take() + fee-on-transfer loanToken + post-maturity sell offer] Can an unprivileged taker under a post-maturity sell offer (block.timestamp > market.maturity) with a fee-on-transfer loanToken call take() where sellerDebtIncrease == 0 is enforced (line 391) but safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) (line 455) still underpays the settlement fee, violating the invariant that claimableSettlementFee[token] <= actual balance surplus even post-maturity, causing scoped impact: post-maturity unwinding via take() compounds settlement fee insolvency? [File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: repay() + IRepayCallback.onRepay() + fee-on-transfer loanToken + callback is payer] Can an unprivileged borrower under a fee-on-transfer loanToken market call repay(market, units, onBehalf, callback, data) with a callback so that payer = callback (line 511) and safeTransferFrom(loanToken, callback, address(this), units) (line 520) delivers only units*(1-fee_rate) while position[id][onBehalf].debt is reduced by units (line 508) and marketState[id].withdrawable is increased by units (line 509), violating the invariant that withdrawable <= loanToken.balanceOf(midnight), causing scoped impact: repay via callback with fee-on-transfer token inflates withdrawable beyond actual balance? [File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: take() + ERC777 loanToken + tokensReceived hook on receiver reenters withdraw()] Can an unprivileged taker under a market whose loanToken is ERC777 trigger a tokensReceived hook on the receiver during safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, receiver, sellerAssets) (line 456) that reenters withdraw() for a lender, so that the lender's withdraw() executes while the outer take() has already updated totalUnits and withdrawable (lines 416-417) but before the outer take()'s health check (line 476), violating the invariant that withdraw() must only execute when withdrawable reflects a fully settled state, causing scoped impact: lender withdraws against a partially settled take state, potentially draining more than available? [File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: supplyCollateral() + fee-on-transfer collateralToken + liquidate() seizes overcounted collateral] Can an unprivileged borrower under a fee-on-transfer collateralToken market call supplyCollateral() to record assets in position.collateral[i] (line 533) while only assets*(1-fee_rate) is received, then become unhealthy, so that liquidate() computes seizedAssets based on the overcounted position.collateral[i] value (line 670) and calls safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, seizedAssets) (line 696) attempting to send more collateral than the protocol holds, violating the invariant that seizedAssets <= collateralToken.balanceOf(midnight), causing scoped impact: liquidation reverts or sends less than seizedAssets if collateral balance is insufficient, freezing liquidation? [File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: take() + fee-on-transfer loanToken + multiple partial fills of same offer] Can an unprivileged taker under a fee-on-transfer loanToken market execute N partial fills of the same offer so that claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] is incremented by (buyerAssets_i - sellerAssets_i) on each fill (line 418) while the protocol receives only (buyerAssets_i - sellerAssets_i)*(1-fee_rate) per fill, causing the insolvency gap to grow linearly with N fills, violating the invariant that claimableSettlementFee[token] <= actual balance surplus, causing scoped impact: high-volume markets with fee-on-transfer loanTokens accumulate unbounded insolvency proportional to trading volume? Proof idea: fuzz N in [1, 100]; fuzz units per fill; assert insolvency gap = N * (buyerAssets - sellerAssets) * fee_rate.

[File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: take() + fee-on-transfer loanToken + offer.buy=true + taker is seller + receiver is taker] Can an unprivileged taker acting as seller under a buy offer (offer.buy=true) with a fee-on-transfer loanToken call take() so that safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, taker, sellerAssets) (line 456, receiver=receiverIfTakerIsSeller=taker) delivers only sellerAssets*(1-fee_rate) to the taker while the taker's debt is increased by sellerDebtIncrease units (line 414) at face value, violating the invariant that the taker receives sellerAssets loanToken in exchange for taking on sellerDebtIncrease debt units, causing scoped impact: taker/borrower receives less loanToken than the debt they incur, creating an unfavorable trade with no protocol recourse? Proof idea: fuzz sellerAssets and fee_rate; assert taker.balanceOf after take < sellerAssets; assert taker.debt == sellerDebtIncrease.
